rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Expunge Pltf'S Lis Pendens And Request To File A Motion To Recover Her Attorney'S Fees And Costs From Pltf Per Ccp 405.38
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 24, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI'S Motion To Expunge Plaintiff's Lis Pendens And Request To File A Motion To Recover Her Attorney's Fees And Costs From Plaintiff Per C.C.P. 405.38 is granted. Plaintiff failed to comply with the timing provisions of C.C.P. section 405.22. The complaint does not plead a real property claim. The body of the U.F.T.A. cause of action alleges fraudulent transfer of moneys, causes of actions, and contracts. Plaintiff fails to show the probable validity of the cause of action. Defendant to recover $1,200 as reasonable fees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: POTRERO MEDIA CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATIO VS. MATTHEW JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 24, 2010, line 10. Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Temporary Restraining Order. Motion granted in part. Plaintiff has shown that the balance of equities weighs in its favor and a liklihood of success on the merits for a claim for misappropriation of trade secrets. The Court would strike "e" from the proposed preliminary injunction, that restrains defendants from "disparaging P.M.C. or its officers or employees." Bond shall be posted in the amount of $10,000 due to failure of defendants to more adequately address the issue of damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: POTRERO MEDIA CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATIO VS. MATTHEW JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Seal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 24, 2010, line 10. Plaintiff's Motion To Seal denied as to Jones declaration paragraph 20, and paragraph 26, lines 3-14 ("as attested... my personal account.") Denied as to opposition page 5, lines 11-13 ("a trade secret... 134.") and lines 16-17 (all methods... trade secret.") Motion granted in all other respects. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 24, 2010, line 15. Order To Show Cause Re Intervention. Granted. Proposed interveners have shown they possess direct and immediate interest in the action that outweighs opposition by plaintiffs. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HASINA, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. CLIFT HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE
